data.name: . If they match, your ISO is valid.
data.value: . If they match, your ISO is valid.

data.name: After downloading the ISO file, verify its integrity to ensure it has not been tampered with.
data.value: After downloading the ISO file, verify its integrity to ensure it has not been tampered with.

data.name: After downloading, you may want to learn how to
data.value: After downloading, you might like to learn how to

data.name: AnduinOS is community-supported. Your donation helps keep development active and independent.
data.value: AnduinOS is community-supported. Your donation helps keep development active and independent.

data.name: BalenaEtcher is a cross-platform graphical tool that makes flashing ISO images simple and safe.
data.value: BalenaEtcher is a cross-platform graphical tool that makes flashing ISO images simple and safe.

data.name: Boot from USB and follow the graphical installer.
data.value: Boot from USB and follow the graphical installer.

data.name: Boot from USB and select the ISO from Ventoy's menu.
data.value: Boot from USB and select the ISO from Ventoy's menu.

data.name: Browse the full documentation for detailed guides, tips, and troubleshooting.
data.value: Browse the full documentation for detailed guides, tips, and troubleshooting.

data.name: burn the ISO to a USB drive
data.value: transfer the ISO to a USB drive

data.name: Change LBA size to 4K for optimal performance
data.value: Change LBA size to 4K for optimal performance

data.name: Change the system language, regional formats, and add input methods like Chinese Pinyin or Japanese Anthy.
data.value: Change the system language, regional formats, and add input methods such as Chinese Pinyin or Japanese Anthy.

data.name: Click "Flash from file" and select the AnduinOS ISO.
data.value: Click "Flash from file" and select the AnduinOS ISO.

data.name: Complete the MOK enrollment on first boot to enable third-party drivers like NVIDIA and Xbox controllers.
data.value: Complete the MOK enrolment on first boot to enable third-party drivers such as NVIDIA and Xbox controllers.

data.name: Copy the AnduinOS ISO onto the drive like a regular file.
data.value: Copy the AnduinOS ISO onto the drive like a regular file.

data.name: Enable it in BIOS before booting
data.value: Enable it in BIOS before booting

data.name: Feeling generous? AnduinOS works based on donations from users. We are grateful for your support.
data.value: Feeling generous? AnduinOS relies on donations from users. We are grateful for your support.

data.name: First things to set up when you boot into AnduinOS.
data.value: Initial setup steps when you boot into AnduinOS.

data.name: If you are new to AnduinOS, you may also want to check out the
data.value: If you are new to AnduinOS, you might also like to have a look at the

data.name: If you frequently install operating systems, Ventoy lets you keep your USB drive usable for regular storage while still being bootable. Simply copy the ISO file onto the drive and boot.
data.value: If you frequently install operating systems, Ventoy allows you to keep your USB drive usable for regular storage while remaining bootable. Simply copy the ISO file onto the drive and boot.

data.name: Impression is the most user-friendly way to flash the ISO on Linux. Install it via Flatpak:
data.value: Impression is the most user-friendly way to flash the ISO on Linux. Install it via Flatpak:

data.name: In GRUB, select your language and choose
data.value: In GRUB, select your language and choose

data.name: Insert the USB drive and power on your computer.
data.value: Insert the USB drive and power on your computer.

data.name: ISO mode may cause AnduinOS to fail to boot.
data.value: ISO mode may cause AnduinOS to fail to boot.

data.name: No repeated flashing or erasing needed!
data.value: No need for repeated flashing or erasing!

data.name: Press your manufacturer's boot menu key immediately.
data.value: Press your manufacturer's boot menu key immediately.

data.name: Security patches are applied automatically. For feature updates, use the App Store's offline update mechanism.
data.value: Security patches are applied automatically. For feature updates, use the App Store's offline update mechanism.

data.name: Select the USB drive in the boot menu.
data.value: Select the USB drive in the boot menu.

data.name: Select your target USB drive and click "Flash!".
data.value: Select your target USB drive and click "Flash!".

data.name: Something not working? Have a suggestion? Join the discussion on GitHub.
data.value: Is something not working? Have a suggestion? Join the discussion on GitHub.

data.name: Something not working? Have a suggestion? Post about it to discussion.
data.value: Something not working? Got a suggestion? Post about it on the discussion forum.

data.name: Take a look at documentation, you may find your answers there.
data.value: Have a look at the documentation; you may find your answers there.

data.name: The AnduinOS graphical installer — straightforward and guided.
data.value: The AnduinOS graphical installer — straightforward and guided.

data.name: UFW is disabled by default (no open ports = no risk). Enable it via the Welcome Center or graphical Firewall app when needed.
data.value: UFW is disabled by default (no open ports = no risk). Enable it via the Welcome Centre or graphical Firewall app when needed.

data.name: Use dd mode, not ISO mode!
data.value: Use dd mode, not ISO mode!

data.name: Use Deja Dup for automated, encrypted backups to an external drive or cloud storage. Set it once, forget about it.
data.value: Use Deja Dup for automated, encrypted backups to an external drive or cloud storage. Set it up once, then forget about it.

data.name: Use the Welcome Center to install NVIDIA drivers, Xbox controller support, and other hardware drivers with one click.
data.value: Use the Welcome Centre to install NVIDIA drivers, Xbox controller support, and other hardware drivers with one click.

data.name: Verify your download and create a bootable USB drive.
data.value: Verify your download and create a bootable USB drive.

data.name: We recommend using Rufus to create the bootable USB drive. Make sure to select
data.value: We recommend using Rufus to create the bootable USB drive. Make sure to select

data.name: Your AnduinOS journey starts here. Follow the steps below to get up and running in under 30 minutes.
data.value: Your AnduinOS journey starts here. Follow the steps below to get up and running in under 30 minutes.
